Fase 1 — Orientasi
Bab 1 · Descriptive Text · People, Things & Places · Kelas X Fase E

Descriptive Text:
Describing People, Things, and Places

Descriptive text helps us make readers or listeners “see” something through words. In this chapter, you will learn to describe a person, an object, and a place clearly using simple English, rich vocabulary, strong details, and local context from Indonesian island life.

Pertanyaan pemantik:
Jika teman dari luar pulau belum pernah melihat Sapuka, bagaimana kamu menjelaskan sekolahmu, dermaga, pantai, tali rumput laut, rumah, perahu, atau orang yang kamu kagumi agar mereka bisa membayangkannya?
ListeningSpeakingReadingViewingWritingPresenting
Fase 1 — Orientasi
🧭 Learning Map

Peta Belajar Bab Ini

Materi ini mencakup seluruh elemen pembelajaran Bahasa Inggris Fase E: menyimak, berbicara, membaca, memirsa, menulis, dan mempresentasikan. Fokus bab ini adalah Descriptive Text tentang orang, benda, dan tempat. Beberapa teks dibuat pendek agar cocok untuk beginner, dan beberapa teks dibuat lebih panjang agar siswa berlatih membaca deskripsi yang lebih lengkap.

ListeningMenyimak deskripsi orang, benda, dan tempat dari guru/TTS/audio.
SpeakingBertanya, menjawab, memberi opini, membandingkan, dan mendeskripsikan dengan jelas.
ReadingMembaca model descriptive text dan menemukan ide pokok, detail, tujuan penulis, serta informasi tersirat.
ViewingMemahami gambar, poster, profil digital, dan foto sebagai teks visual/multimodal.
WritingMenulis deskripsi orang, benda, dan tempat melalui proses plan → draft → review → redraft.
PresentingMempresentasikan deskripsi dengan suara, intonasi, gesture, dan media sederhana.
GrammarMenggunakan simple present, be, has/have, there is/are, adjectives, prepositions, dan comparison.
Digital LiteracyMembuat caption, poster text, profile card, atau short presentation script.
Fase 1 — Orientasi
🎯 Tujuan Pembelajaran

Learning Objectives

ElemenYang Siswa Kuasai dalam Bab Descriptive Text
ListeningMemahami ide utama dan detail dari deskripsi lisan tentang orang, benda, dan tempat; merespons pertanyaan sederhana.
SpeakingMendeskripsikan orang, benda, dan tempat; memberi opini; membuat perbandingan; mempertahankan percakapan sederhana.
ReadingMengidentifikasi identification, description, main idea, specific details, author’s purpose, dan simple inference.
ViewingMembaca detail visual dari gambar/foto/poster dan mengubahnya menjadi kalimat deskriptif.
WritingMenulis descriptive text dengan struktur jelas, vocabulary tepat, simple present, capital letters, punctuation, dan revisi mandiri.
PresentingMenyajikan deskripsi dalam bentuk lisan, poster, profile card, atau digital caption sesuai tujuan dan audiens.
💡Prinsip bab ini: siswa tidak hanya menghafal definisi descriptive text, tetapi belajar melihat, memilih detail penting, menyusun kalimat, berbicara, menulis, merevisi, dan mempresentasikan.
Fase 1 — Orientasi
🖼️ Picture Input

Picture Gallery for Descriptive Text

Gambar berikut digunakan sebagai bahan mengamati, menyimak, berbicara, membaca gambar, menulis, dan presentasi. Catatan: gambar bersifat ilustratif untuk latihan pembelajaran, bukan dokumentasi faktual tempat tertentu.

Use for: describing people, personality adjectives, speaking practice, and profile writing.
Use for: describing places, atmosphere, facilities, location, and comparison.
Use for: describing objects, color, shape, material, condition, and function.
Fase 2 — Konsep Inti
📘 Social Function & Structure

What Is Descriptive Text?

Descriptive text is a text that describes a particular person, thing, animal, or place. Its purpose is to help the reader imagine the object clearly. In Indonesian, descriptive text membantu pembaca “melihat” sesuatu melalui kata-kata.

StructureFunctionExample
IdentificationIntroduces the specific person, thing, or place.My school is SMA Negeri 6 Pangkajene dan Kepulauan.
DescriptionGives details: appearance, qualities, parts, location, function, atmosphere, and opinion.It has simple classrooms, a yard, and a flagpole. The students are friendly and hardworking.
Personphysical appearance, personality, clothes, actions, reason for admiration.
Thing/Objectsize, color, shape, material, condition, parts, function, importance.
Placelocation, view, facilities, atmosphere, people, activities, personal feeling.
Fase 2 — Vocabulary Building
🧠 Expanded Vocabulary Bank

Kosakata untuk Mendeskripsikan Lebih Lengkap

Kosakata di bawah ini diperbanyak agar siswa tidak selalu memakai kata yang sama seperti good, nice, atau beautiful. Guru dapat memilih kosakata dasar terlebih dahulu untuk siswa beginner, lalu menambah kata-kata baru secara bertahap. Di setiap bagian, siswa dianjurkan tidak hanya menghafal arti kata, tetapi juga mencoba menggunakannya dalam kalimat sederhana, perbandingan, dan deskripsi yang lebih lengkap.

A. Describing People

CategoryVocabularySimple Sentence
Appearancetall, short, slim, strong, athletic, neat, tidy, elegant, handsome, pretty, young, elderly, healthy, energeticHe is tall, neat, and energetic.
Hair / Faceshort hair, long hair, curly hair, straight hair, black hair, bright eyes, calm face, warm smile, round face, sharp noseShe has long black hair and a warm smile.
Personalitykind, patient, friendly, diligent, humble, brave, cheerful, honest, responsible, creative, disciplined, helpful, polite, caring, confident, independent, curiousMy friend is diligent, polite, and responsible.
Study Habitshardworking, focused, active, serious, organized, prepared, punctual, consistent, cooperativeRafi is hardworking and always prepared for class.
Opinion Wordsinspiring, admirable, impressive, reliable, supportive, trustworthy, memorableMy English teacher is inspiring and supportive.

B. Describing Things

CategoryVocabularySimple Sentence
Size / Shapesmall, big, tiny, large, long, short, wide, narrow, round, square, flat, thin, thick, curvedThe rope is long, strong, and quite thick.
Color / Materialblue, black, brown, green, white, colorful, wooden, plastic, metal, cotton, nylon, leather, rubberThe boat is blue and wooden.
Conditionnew, old, clean, dirty, simple, modern, traditional, strong, broken, useful, practical, heavy, light, soft, hard, durableMy bag is old but still useful and durable.
Functioncarry, keep, protect, tie, hold, store, support, cover, help, containI use the bag to carry and protect my books.
Opinion Wordsimportant, helpful, valuable, meaningful, ordinary, special, favorite, convenientThis simple rope is very important for seaweed farmers.

C. Describing Places

CategoryVocabularySimple Sentence
Locationnear, beside, behind, in front of, between, across from, next to, on the coast, in the village, on the island, at the end of the roadThe school is near the beach and beside the main road.
Natural Viewclear water, white sand, green hill, rocky shore, wooden pier, blue sky, tall trees, small houses, coral reef, open seaThere are small houses near the green hill.
Atmospherepeaceful, quiet, crowded, busy, fresh, hot, windy, bright, relaxing, remote, special, calm, lively, comfortable, shadyThe pier is quiet in the morning but lively in the afternoon.
Facilities / Partsclassrooms, library, school yard, mosque, market, harbor, bridge, garden, field, canteen, flagpoleMy school has classrooms, a yard, and a flagpole.
Opinion Wordsbeautiful, memorable, meaningful, welcoming, attractive, interesting, impressiveThe beach is beautiful and very memorable for me.

D. Useful Intensifiers

a little
The classroom is a little small, but it is comfortable.
quite
The beach is quite peaceful in the early morning.
very
My teacher is very patient and helpful.
really
The place is really special for our community.
extremely
The weather is extremely hot at noon.
fairly
The market is fairly crowded before sunset.

E. Prepositions and Position Phrases

Preposition / PhraseUseExample
ininside a spaceThe books are in the bag.
ontouching a surfaceThe notebook is on the table.
underlower than somethingThe shoes are under the chair.
near / close tonot far fromMy house is near the school.
beside / next toat the side of somethingThe flagpole is beside the office.
behindat the back of somethingThe garden is behind the classroom.
in front ofbefore somethingThe students stand in front of the class.
betweenin the middle of two thingsThe library is between the office and the canteen.
across fromon the opposite sideThe market is across from the pier.
aroundsurrounding somethingThere are coconut trees around the beach.
alongfollowing a line or sideMany boats are parked along the shore.
at the end ofthe last point of a placeThere is a small hut at the end of the pier.
Fase 2 — Input
🎧 Listening Zone

Listening: Describing People, Things, and Places

Dengarkan teks pertama untuk gambaran umum, lalu dengarkan lagi untuk detail. Teks berikut sengaja dibuat dengan panjang yang berbeda: ada yang pendek, sedang, dan lebih panjang. Guru dapat membacakan teks dengan intonasi yang jelas atau siswa dapat memakai tombol TTS. Setelah mendengarkan, siswa sebaiknya menandai kata sifat, kata benda, dan frasa lokasi yang muncul.

Listening Text 1 — A Hardworking Student Short
Rafi is a hardworking student in my class. He is not the loudest student, but he is serious about his lessons and respectful to his teachers. He has short black hair, a neat uniform, and a small black bag that he brings every day. Every morning, he comes to school on time, listens carefully, and writes important notes in his notebook. After class, he often helps his friends when they do not understand the lesson. I admire him because he is responsible, humble, and always willing to learn.
Questions:
  • Who is described?
  • Mention two adjectives about Rafi.
  • What things does he bring to school?
  • Why does the writer admire him?
Lihat Contoh Jawaban

Rafi is described. Possible adjectives: hardworking, serious, responsible, humble, neat. He brings a small black bag and a notebook. The writer admires him because he studies seriously and helps his friends.

Listening Text 2 — My English Teacher Medium
My English teacher is one of the most inspiring people in my school. Her name is Miss Lina. She is not very tall, but she looks confident, friendly, and always ready to help. She usually wears a neat uniform, carries a notebook, and begins the lesson with a warm smile. When she smiles, the classroom feels warmer and more comfortable. Miss Lina is patient when students make mistakes. She explains difficult words slowly, gives examples from daily life, and often asks us to practice English in pairs. She also encourages shy students to speak. I like her because she makes English feel less scary, more useful, and more connected to our future dreams.
Questions:
  • What is the text mainly about?
  • What does Miss Lina look like?
  • How does she teach difficult words?
  • What does she do for shy students?
  • What does “English feels less scary” mean?
Lihat Contoh Jawaban

It is about the writer’s English teacher. She is confident, friendly, and neat. She explains difficult words slowly and gives daily-life examples. She encourages shy students to speak. “English feels less scary” means students feel more confident and less afraid of making mistakes.

Listening Text 3 — The Small Pier in My Village Longer
There is a small pier near my house. It is not modern, but it is very important for the people in my village. The pier is made of wood and concrete. Some parts look old because the sun, rain, and sea wind touch it every day. In the morning, many people stand there to wait for boats. Some carry baskets, some carry school bags, and some bring goods from the market. In the afternoon, children sometimes sit near the pier and watch the sea, while fishermen prepare their boats. The sea around the pier is sometimes calm and sometimes windy. At sunset, the place looks beautiful because the sky becomes orange and the water shines softly. For me, the pier is not only a place for boats. It is also a place where people say goodbye, wait with hope, share stories, and welcome their family home.
Questions:
  • What place is described?
  • What is the pier made of?
  • Why do some parts look old?
  • What activities happen there in the morning and afternoon?
  • Why is the pier special for the writer?
Lihat Contoh Jawaban

The text describes a small pier. It is made of wood and concrete. Some parts look old because of the sun, rain, and sea wind. In the morning people wait for boats; in the afternoon children and fishermen gather there. It is special because it is full of memories, hope, and family moments.

Fase 3 — Guided Practice
🗣️ Speaking Zone

Speaking: Ask, Answer, Describe, and Compare

Non-verbal focus: Students practice clear voice, eye contact, gestures, confidence, and smile.

A. Useful Questions

TopicQuestionsAnswer Starters
PersonWhat does he/she look like? What is he/she like? Why do you admire him/her?He is... / She has... / I admire her because...
ThingWhat is it? What does it look like? What is it used for?It is... / It has... / It is used to...
PlaceWhere is it? What can you see there? What is the atmosphere like?It is located... / There is... / The place feels...

B. Dialogue Practice — Describing a Place

Mira: What is your favorite place in your village? Dina: My favorite place is the beach near my house. Mira: What is it like? Dina: It is quiet, windy, and beautiful. There are small boats near the shore. Mira: Why do you like it? Dina: I like it because the air is fresh and the view makes me calm.

C. Comparison Practice

PlacesWhich place looks more peaceful: the beach or the market? Why?
ObjectsWhich object is more useful for students: a school bag or a textbook? Why?
PeopleWho is more inspiring for you: a teacher, a student, or a fisherman? Why?
Sentence FrameI think ... is more ... than ... because ...

D. Speaking Mini Task

Choose one picture from the posters. Speak for 45–60 seconds using this frame:

I want to describe picture number ... It shows ... I can see ... The person/place/object looks ... There is/There are ... In my opinion, ... because ...
Fase 3 — Reading Input
📖 Reading Zone

Reading: Model Texts with Different Lengths

Teks berikut sengaja dibuat bervariasi. Ada teks pendek untuk latihan awal, teks sedang untuk memperkuat pemahaman, dan teks lebih panjang untuk membangun stamina membaca.

Text 1 — A Kind Fisherman Short

A kind fisherman in my village is Pak Amir. He is an elderly man with dark skin, a warm smile, and strong hands. He usually wears a cap and a simple shirt. Every morning, he prepares his fishing tools carefully before going to sea. Pak Amir is friendly and helpful. He works hard to catch fish, but he also takes care of his family and helps his neighbors when they need support. When he talks to young people, he speaks gently and gives useful advice. I respect him because he shows that a good person can be simple, humble, hardworking, and useful to others.
  1. Who is described?
  2. Mention three adjectives from the text.
  3. Why does the writer respect Pak Amir?

Text 2 — A Seaweed Rope Medium

A seaweed rope is an important object for many families in Sapuka. It is usually long, strong, and simple. Some ropes are blue, black, or green. They are not expensive, but they are very useful for daily work. The rope is used to tie seaweed seedlings before they are placed in the sea. Because it stays in seawater for many days, the rope must be durable and practical. When the sea is good and the weather is dry, the seaweed can grow well on the rope. Many students know this object because they sometimes help their parents tie seaweed seedlings after school or during the weekend. For some people, it is only a rope. For seaweed farmers, however, it is part of their work, hope, routine, and daily life.
  1. What object is described?
  2. What does it look like?
  3. What is it used for?
  4. Why is it important for families?
  5. What is the writer’s opinion?

Text 3 — My School on Sapuka Island Longer

My school is SMA Negeri 6 Pangkajene dan Kepulauan. It is located on Sapuka Island, a remote island in South Sulawesi. The school is not very large compared with schools in the city, but it is very meaningful for students from the surrounding islands. From the outside, the school looks simple and neat. There are classrooms, an office, a yard, a flagpole, and several learning spaces for students and teachers. Some classrooms are plain, but they are useful and full of learning activities. In the morning, sunlight enters through the windows, and the school yard becomes bright. Students usually come by walking from their homes. Some students arrive after helping their families, especially during the seaweed season. The teachers work patiently because they know that learning on an island has many challenges, such as distance, weather, and limited facilities. For me, this school is special. It is not only a building. It is a place where students from a remote island can build knowledge, confidence, friendship, discipline, and hope for the future.
  1. Where is the school located?
  2. How is the school different from schools in the city?
  3. What facilities are mentioned?
  4. Why do teachers need to be patient?
  5. What does “a place of hope” mean?

Text 4 — My Black School Bag Short

My favorite object is my black school bag. It is not new, but it is still strong and useful. The bag has two large pockets and one small pocket. I use it to carry my books, pens, ruler, and water bottle. The straps are soft, so it is comfortable to carry, even when the bag is heavy. I like my bag because it helps me prepare for school every day and keeps my things safe and organized.

Text 5 — The Small Pier at Sunset Longer

The small pier near my village is one of the most memorable places for me. It stretches into the sea like a narrow wooden path. In the afternoon, the pier becomes a meeting place for children, fishermen, and families who wait for boats. The wood is old in some parts, but it is still strong. Around the pier, there are small boats floating on the calm water. Some people sit quietly, while others talk and watch the waves. When the sun begins to set, the sky changes from blue to orange, then slowly becomes purple. The sea reflects the color of the sky, and the wind feels cooler. The pier is not luxurious, but it has many stories. People leave the island from this place, and people also return home here. That is why I think the pier is more than a structure. It is a quiet place of memory, waiting, reunion, and hope.

Structure Analysis

QuestionGuided Answer
Which sentence introduces the object/place/person?This is usually the identification.
Which sentences give details?These sentences form the description.
Which words describe qualities?Find adjectives: simple, strong, calm, remote, bright, useful, patient.
Which sentence gives the writer’s opinion?Look for: I like..., I respect..., For me..., I think...
Fase 3 — Viewing
👁️ Viewing Pictures

Viewing: Read the Picture Before You Write

Viewing berarti memahami gambar, foto, layout, warna, ekspresi, posisi, dan pesan visual. Di bagian ini, siswa belajar mengambil detail dari gambar sebelum menulis deskripsi. Guru dapat meminta siswa mengamati objek, suasana, aktivitas, warna, serta letak benda di dalam gambar, lalu mengubah hasil pengamatan itu menjadi kalimat deskriptif yang lebih lengkap.

Task: Choose two pictures. For each picture, write at least 5 nouns, 8 adjectives, and 4 descriptive sentences. Then explain the location using prepositions such as near, behind, between, and in front of.

Viewing Questions

ObjectsWhat can you see? How many objects are there?
PlaceWhere is the place? Is it near the sea, school, hill, or village?
Color & MoodWhat colors can you see? Does the place feel calm, busy, bright, or quiet?

Sentence Starters

  • I can see ...
  • There is a ...
  • There are many ...
  • The place looks ...
  • The people look ...
  • The picture makes me feel ...

Picture Story for Description

Task: Describe the student’s daily life in a complete paragraph. Notice that he does not wear his school uniform in every activity. In the morning he wakes up at home, gets ready, and later goes to school in uniform. After school he changes into casual clothes to help his father with seaweed, and at night he studies and reads at home. Use adjectives such as diligent, disciplined, helpful, responsible, and independent.
Fase 4 — Grammar Lab
🧪 Grammar Lab

Language Features of Descriptive Text

A. Simple Present Tense

PatternExample
Subject + is/am/are + adjectiveThe beach is beautiful. I am proud of my school. The students are friendly.
Subject + has/have + nounMy school has classrooms. We have a small library.
Subject + Verb 1 / Verb-sShe teaches English. The boat carries people.

B. To Be: is / am / are

I amI am proud of my village.
He/She/It isShe is patient. It is useful.
You/We/They areThey are helpful.

C. Has / Have

HasMy school has a yard. She has a warm smile.
HaveWe have many books. They have small boats.

D. There is / There are

There is + singularThere is a flagpole in front of the school.
There are + pluralThere are many boats near the pier.

E. Prepositions of Place

Practice: Make sentences using in, on, under, near, beside, behind, between, in front of, and across from. Then describe where objects are located in your classroom or house.
More examples:
  • The school library is between the office and the classroom.
  • There is a flagpole in front of the school yard.
  • My bag is under the table.
  • The market is across from the harbor.
  • There are coconut trees around the beach.
  • Several boats are parked along the shore.

F. Adjective Order — Simple Version

PatternExample
size + color + nouna small blue boat
condition + material + nounan old wooden pier
opinion + nouna beautiful beach, a useful rope

G. Comparison for Simple Opinion

-er thanMy classroom is smaller than the library.
more ... thanThe pier is more peaceful than the market.
as ... asThe seaweed rope is as useful as a fishing net.
Fase 4 — Accuracy
⚠️ Common Mistakes

Kesalahan yang Sering Muncul

Wrong
She beautiful.
My school have a library.
There is many boats.
He is have short hair.
The beach very clean.
Correct
She is beautiful.
My school has a library.
There are many boats.
He has short hair.
The beach is very clean.
📝Untuk siswa beginner, fokus koreksi utama cukup pada is/am/are, has/have, there is/are, kapital, titik, dan pemilihan adjective.
Fase 5 — Guided Practice
✍️ Guided Practice

Practice Before Writing

Practice 1 — Complete the Description

Complete the text with suitable words.

My classroom is not very ___, but it is clean and comfortable. There are many ___ and chairs. The whiteboard is on the ___. There is a teacher’s desk in ___ of the class. I like my classroom because it is a good place for ___.

Word bank: wall — big — front — studying — tables

big, tables, wall, front, studying

Practice 2 — Improve the Sentence

Too GeneralBetter Description
My school is good.My school is simple, clean, and important for students on the island.
My friend is nice.My friend is kind, cheerful, helpful, and responsible.
The beach is beautiful.The beach is quiet and beautiful, with blue water, soft sand, and small boats.
My bag is useful.My black school bag is useful because it carries my books every day.

Practice 3 — Build a Paragraph

Topic: My Favorite Place 1. Identification: My favorite place is ... 2. Location: It is located ... 3. Details: It has ... / There is ... / There are ... 4. Atmosphere: The place feels ... 5. Opinion: I like it because ...
Fase 5 — Check Understanding
🧩 Interactive Quiz

Quick Quiz

Choose the best answer. Your score will appear at the end.

0/0
Fase 6 — Writing Mission
📝 Writing Zone

Writing Descriptive Text

Siswa menulis melalui proses: plan → draft → review → redraft. Guru dapat memberi pilihan topik agar siswa menulis sesuai kemampuan dan pengalaman mereka.

Choose a topic: classroom, library, flagpole, school yard, mosque, house, traditional boat, seaweed harvest, local market, waterfall, mountain, or Borobudur Temple.

A. Writing Options

Beginner8–10 simple sentences. Use many sentence starters.
Intermediate2–3 paragraphs, 120–180 words, with adjectives and opinion.
Challenge180–230 words, include comparison and personal reflection.

B. Writing Template

Title: ____________________ Paragraph 1 — Identification I want to describe ... It/He/She is ... It/He/She is located ... Paragraph 2 — Description It/He/She has ... There is ... / There are ... It/He/She looks ... The most interesting thing is ... Paragraph 3 — Opinion / Closing I like/respect/admire ... because ... For me, ... is ...

C. Student Writing Box

0 words

D. Self-Editing Checklist

Fase 7 — Presenting
🎤 Presenting Zone

Present Your Description

Siswa dapat mempresentasikan teks dalam bentuk oral presentation, poster, photo description, voice note script, short video script, atau digital profile.

Digital products: social media caption, digital profile, and mini blog. Use these as examples for presenting descriptive text in digital form.

Presentation Frame

Good morning, everyone. Today, I want to describe ... It/He/She is ... It/He/She has ... There is/There are ... The most interesting thing is ... I like/respect/admire it/him/her because ... Thank you.

Peer Feedback

QuestionFriend’s Answer
What did your friend describe?
Mention two adjectives your friend used.
Was the voice clear?
What was the best part?
Give one suggestion.
Fase 8 — Final Assessment
🏁 Final Task

Final Task — No Answer Key

📌Bagian ini untuk asesmen. Tidak disediakan kunci jawaban agar siswa benar-benar menghasilkan karya sendiri.

Task 1 — Reading

My favorite place is a small beach near my village. The beach is not crowded. It has soft sand, blue water, and several small boats near the shore. In the morning, the air feels fresh and the sea looks calm. I often go there with my friends after school. We do not swim far because the sea can be dangerous. We usually sit near the boats and talk about school. I like this place because it makes me feel peaceful.
  1. What place is described?
  2. What does the beach look like?
  3. When does the air feel fresh?
  4. Why does the writer like the place?
  5. Find five adjectives from the text.

Task 2 — Writing

Write a descriptive text about one person, one thing, or one place. Minimum 120 words. Include a title, identification, description, at least 8 adjectives, simple present tense, and personal opinion.

Task 3 — Speaking / Presenting

Present your descriptive text in 1–2 minutes. Introduce your topic, describe clearly, use at least five adjectives, speak clearly, use good eye contact, and close politely.

Assessment Rubric
📊 Rubric

Rubrik Penilaian

A. Writing Rubric

AspectExcellentGoodNeeds Support
StructureClear identification and descriptionMostly clearConfusing
ContentRich and specific detailsSome clear detailsToo general
GrammarSimple present mostly accurateSome errorsMany errors
VocabularyVaried adjectives and detailsBasic but understandableVery limited
MechanicsGood punctuation and capitalizationSome mistakesMany mistakes

B. Speaking Rubric

AspectExcellentGoodNeeds Support
ContentClear and complete descriptionUnderstandableToo short/unclear
PronunciationClear and easy to understandSome unclear wordsDifficult to understand
FluencyMostly smoothSome pausesToo many pauses
Non-verbalGood eye contact, gesture, voiceSome eye contactReads fully/no eye contact
ConfidenceConfidentFairly confidentVery hesitant
Teacher Notes
🧑‍🏫 Catatan Guru

Teaching Notes for Beginner Students

  • Mulai dari gambar → kata → frasa → kalimat → paragraf.
  • Untuk listening, guru bisa menjadi model audio jika koneksi internet tidak stabil.
  • Untuk siswa pemalu, mulai dari pair work sebelum presentasi kelas.
  • Untuk writing, jangan langsung menuntut teks panjang. Gunakan template dan sentence starters.
  • Untuk konteks Sapuka/Liukang Tangaya, gunakan foto lokal bila tersedia, tetapi gambar ilustratif ini tetap bisa dipakai untuk latihan awal.
IsleLearn English X · Chapter 1 Descriptive Text · Integrated Skills · Offline-friendly HTML
